ocr.text: In the 2Q FY2025 revenue report for Merck & Co within the Asia-Pacific region, the table on Revenue by Therapeutic Area
reveals significant insights across various categories. The Oncology segment reported 15,000 units sold, generating revenue of
$5,000,000,000, supported by a headcount of 200 employees, involvement in 15 clinical trials, and capturing a market share of
25%. The Cardiovascular therapeutic area achieved sales of 12,000 units, resulting in revenue of $4,000,000,000, with a
headcount of 180, participating in 14 trials, and holding a market share of 15.3%. The Neurology sector sold 8,000 units,
translating to revenue of $2,500,000,000, employing 100 staff members, engaging in 10 clinical trials, and maintaining a market
share of 12%. The Diabetes area recorded 11,000 units sold, bringing in $3,500,000,000, with a workforce of 160, involved in
13 trials, and securing a market share of 7%.
RevenuebyTherapeuticArea  UnitsSold  Revenue(KRW)  Headcount  NumberofTrials  MarketShare(%)
Immunology achieved a sale of 9,000 units, which equated to $3,000,000,000 in revenue, supported by a headcount of 120,
with 11 trials conducted, and a market share of 11%. The Respiratory sector reported 10,000 units sold, generating
$3,500,000,000 in revenue, employing 160 people, conducting 13 trials, and capturing a market share of 9%. The Infectious
Disease area, with 7,000 units sold, generated revenue of $2,000,000,000, supported by a headcount of 90, involved in 9 trials,
and holding a market share of 5%. Overall, the Total for all therapeutic areas combined reflects 70,000 units sold, resulting in
total revenue of $23,000,000,000, with a combined headcount of 1,000 employees, 81 trials conducted, and an overall market
share of 45%. This comprehensive overview underscores the diverse performance across therapeutic areas, highlighting both
strengths and areas for potential growth within Merck & Co's strategic focus in the Asia-Pacific market.
